name: Fauna
description: Fauna is a distributed, serverless document-relational database with a native HTTP API. It combines the flexibility of documents with relational features like joins, indexes, and ACID transactions. Query using FQL (Fauna Query Language) over HTTPS with no connection management required.

capabilities.name: run_query
capabilities.description: Execute an FQL (Fauna Query Language) query against your database. Supports reads, writes, aggregations, joins, and complex logic all in a single query. FQL is a TypeScript-like language with full transaction support.

capabilities.name: get_document
capabilities.description: Retrieve a single document from a collection by its ID. Returns the full document with all its fields, metadata, and timestamps.

capabilities.name: create_document
capabilities.description: Create a new document in a collection. Specify the collection name and document data. Fauna automatically assigns a unique ID and tracks creation/update timestamps.

capabilities.name: update_document
capabilities.description: Update an existing document by its ID. Supports partial updates that merge new fields with the existing document, or full replacement of document data.

capabilities.name: delete_document
capabilities.description: Delete a document from a collection by its ID. The operation is immediate and permanent. Returns the deleted document data for confirmation.

capabilities.name: list_documents
capabilities.description: List documents in a collection with optional pagination. Use indexes for filtered or sorted results. Returns documents in pages with a cursor for fetching the next batch.
